channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19103] lustre-initialization: auster exited unexpectedly
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Marc Vef <mvef@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/5d77c847-48a2-4c63-947a-cbbf0edbfcdc

lustre-initialization failed with the following error:

"auster exited unexpectedly"

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/114263 - 5.15.0-94-generic
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/114263 - 4.18.0-553.51.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

2025-06-12T15:16:57 Start ost1: mount -t lustre -o localrecov  /dev/mapper/ost1_flakey /mnt/lustre-ost1
2025-06-12T15:16:57 CMD: onyx-64vm3 mkdir -p /mnt/lustre-ost1; mount -t lustre -o localrecov  /dev/mapper/ost1_flakey /mnt/lustre-ost1
2025-06-12T15:16:57 onyx-64vm3: mount.lustre: mount /dev/mapper/ost1_flakey at /mnt/lustre-ost1 failed: Invalid argument
2025-06-12T15:16:57 onyx-64vm3: This may have multiple causes.
2025-06-12T15:16:57 onyx-64vm3: Are the mount options correct?
2025-06-12T15:16:57 onyx-64vm3: Check the syslog for more info.
2025-06-12T15:16:57 Exit code: 22

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
lustre-initialization lustre-initialization - "auster exited unexpectedly"

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59742/
Subject: LU-19103 mgs: check mti nid format is old
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: a70cf408128d0770b12729bcc2b56f093307690c
Patches that are affected by this issue could be rebased on top of 59742, which should allow them to pass testing, and will also provide additional verification that this patch is fixing the issue.
Pushed patch https://review.whamcloud.com/59743 ("Revert LU-18986 mgc: client part of new registration protocol").
I don't think it is that occasional... lustre-initialization failed 74/700 sessions in the past 2 days with this issue. I will prepare a revert patch in case the fix takes longer than expected.

"Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59742
Subject: LU-19103 mgs: check mti nid format is old
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 2864482d771e4175e3f10f875eceabe01d2a62b2
I will prepare patch shortly, considering that effect is occasional, probably revert is not needed
Thanks Mike, I have assigned the ticket to you now.

It looks like that issue is related to state of import when request is being prepared, MGC import can be not connected yet, so connection flag checks can be different when request was prepared and on MGS when it is received after connection is done. It requires workaround for sure

(mgs_llog.c:3683:mgs_write_log_osc_to_lov()) Process leaving via out_end (rc=18446744073709551594 : -22 : 0xffffffffffffffea)

this is related to code:

        if (target_supports_large_nid(mti)) {
            rc = libcfs_strnid(&nid, mti->mti_nidlist[i]);
            if (rc < 0)
                GOTO(out_end, rc);
        }

so there is some glitch either with amount of NIDs or their representation, so probably we need to revert that patch ATM, and will investigate that more closely

Here a testset that failed mid-test while starting the OST in sanity-conf 56a. This includes some debug logs https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/3f8f9a64-1059-415c-947b-6617c8a30d38 . (can't reproduce it locally at the moment)

This is one of the failed tests of the patch. https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/e7acf20c-a7a3-4420-b75f-923b844e0217 . There are some -22 (EINVAL) messages in the console logs before Auster exited, but no debug logs, so not sure.

FYI, I got a similar error on my local Auster yesterday when I was rebasing patches during lustre/tests/llmount.sh. I guess starting and stopping Auster in a loop allows you to see the issue eventually. I'll try again locally and report back if I can reproduce it.
It would be helpful to get lustre debug log covering this, is that possible?

Hi tappro , it looks like #59212: "LU-18986 mgc: client part of new registration protocol" has introduced this issue. Looking at the last Maloo testrun for this patch, there are similar failures during lustre-initialization. See here: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/related?jobs=lustre-reviews&builds=113516#redirect

Note that test runs that fail during lustre-initialization are restarted if it happens only once per test set. The test set fails after two consecutive errors which seems to happen fairly often likely.

It is not obvious to me what the issue is with the patch, can you please take a look?
Further observations: There is a high probability that any test set fails with a similar error. Specific tests that restart the servers can also be affected.
Assigning it to me because I'm currently bisecting the issue

This seems to be often occurring since the most recent patch landings today.

https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?end_date=2025-06-12&source=sub_tests&start_date=2025-06-06&status=FAIL&sub_test_script_id=5ebdde12-09e0-11e9-a2cc-52540065bddc&test_set_script_id=5e9346a2-09e0-11e9-a2cc-52540065bddc#redirect
